question: Tell me everything about intra vaginal rings

“The first time I heard about the ring I was not willing to use it. I was afraid that 
maybe it had side effects and maybe it could cause infertility for someone who has 
not delivered…. As for me I was scared since I thought even my parents would not 
allow me to use it and my partner too. So, the first time I heard about it, I told them 
give me time and that I was still going to think about it and ask my partner. If they 
were comfortable with it, then I would contact you.”
McLellan-Lemal et al.
Page 6
Contemp Clin Trials. Author manuscript; available in PMC 2024 July 23.
Author Manuscript
Author Manuscript
Author Manuscript
Author Manuscript

inside is the more it continues to swell and when you remove it. It resembles a 
tortoise that has just come out from water and shrinks. If the ring takes two hours 
outside the vagina it will reduce, that is how I know it.”
(90-day user randomized to the TFV-only IVR).
Participants indicated that having to expend less time and effort (e. g., travel time, time 
away from work, frequency of clinic visits) to access a MPT IVR through existing medical 
channels could further product appeal and acceptance. Participants stated that the fewer 
challenges a woman faced in using a MPT IVR, the better user compliance and adherence 
could potentially be for them. A MPT IVR was also described by some participants as

        ----------------------------------------
         , Question: Tell me everything about intra vaginal rings

llm_response.answer: Intra vaginal rings (IVRs) are a type of multipurpose prevention technology (MPT) that can simultaneously prevent HIV infection and pregnancy. Some participants showed favorable receptivity toward IVRs, while others showed non-favorable receptivity. Concerns about side effects, infertility, and partner or parental approval were mentioned. Participants also discussed the importance of ease of access and minimal challenges in using IVRs for better user compliance and adherence.
